title: Mantis Syndicate
content:

}}
{{Quote|Not particularly subtle, Mantis Syndicate operatives were frequently employed to capture or otherwise eliminate criminal organizations, pirate gangs, and Alliance units operating in Sarin sector, especially those groups whose operations ran counter to Lord Affric's interests.|Captain Zgorth'sth, Department of Threat Assessment, New Republic|<ref name="SM" />}}
The '''Mantis Syndicate''', or '''Mantis Bounty Hunter Syndicate''', was a corporation of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and one of the houses belonging to the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]]. Officially specializing in hunting big groups of criminals such as [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] or [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] organizations, the Mantis Syndicate gathered great numbers of hunters in [[Battalion/Legends|battalions]] of military units to carry out assignments. Detractors considered the Mantis Syndicate to be a company of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]].

The Mantis Syndicate was active from at least [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Mantis Syndicate was owned and managed by [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Marina Mantis]]. By then, the Mantis Syndicate worked mostly as a private [[Army/Legends|army]] for [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[High Lord]] [[Jaris Affric]] of the [[Sarin sector/Legends|Sarin sector]], although the Syndicate also reached the [[Revkinn system/Legends|Revkinn]] and [[Socorro system/Legends|Socorro systems]]. By the end of the war, Affric had suffered a loss of income, and the Mantis Syndicate terminated its working relationship with him. Lady Mantis then made deals with other customers, including the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and Provisional Governor [[Valis Lorn]] of [[Andasala]].

==Organization==
The Mantis [[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty Hunter]] Syndicate, commonly known as the Mantis Syndicate, was a private company<ref name="GG10" /> working in the business of [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] legally issued by either corporate or government interests.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> The Mantis Syndicate was one of the ten principal houses forming the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]].<ref name="UG">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> The owner and chief executive [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the Syndicate was [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Marina Mantis]] from at least [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]<ref name="GG10" /> to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],<ref name="SM" /> and the Syndicate's offices were established on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Santarine]]. The Syndicate's logo was a white stylized "M" within a black circle.<ref name="Code" />

Professionals were able to join the Mantis Syndicate either by passing a selection process, or by being personally chosen and recruited by Lady Mantis. The Syndicate, like the other houses, offered training and support to its members. In exchange, every bounty hunter paid the Syndicate a fee, including 3–5% of any collected bounty, depending on the value of the bounty and the seniority of the hunter, plus an annual fee of 150 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Hunters recruited by Lady Mantis were exempt from the annual fee.<ref name="GG10" />

The Syndicate worked on general-purpose bounties, but its members specialized in high-risk missions<ref name="GG10" /> and in paramilitary operations requiring literally a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of professionals<ref name="GG9" /> to track down and capture collective prey such as a gang or a military unit.<ref name="SM" /> One of the main appeals of the Syndicate was the fact that it gathered specialized [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], all of them experienced as bounty hunters, in large numbers and in little [[Time/Legends|time]] for a given assignment.<ref name="GG10" /> Detractors believed that this feature blurred the difference between a bounty hunter syndicate and a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] company.<ref name="GG9" />

Some members of the Mantis Syndicate operated solo,<ref name="BS" /> but as a norm they made four- to eight-person units. Those groups offered a teamwork synergy that the Syndicate found valuable. The Mantis Syndicate succeeded in seventeen out of every twenty assignments.<ref name="SM" />

==History==
The Mantis Syndicate was active circa [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]]. At that point, Mantis sent a prominent bounty hunter, [[Fenn Rizaar]], to track down [[Kaine Paulsen]], a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and member of the criminal organization [[Black Bha'lir]]. During the chase, Rizaar accidentally shot an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Death/Legends|dead]] and, shortly afterward, was apprehended by his victim's [[Platoon/Legends|platoon]] along with Paulsen. Paulsen and Rizaar escaped together and went to the Bha'lir headquarters on [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]. The Mantis Syndicate then refused to acknowledge Rizaar as one of its members and offered a bounty of 70,000 credits for his head. Some members of the Black Bha'lir wanted to cash in Rizaar, but Paulsen convinced them not to. Rizaar believed he was indebted to the Bha'lir and eventually became a Bha'lir himself. He also swore revenge on the Mantis Syndicate.<ref name="BS" />

The Mantis Syndicate was still active and a member of the Bounty Hunters' Guild as of [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]].<ref name="TFU">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Soon after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Galactic Empire showed a sudden interest in criminal and fringe organizations, including bounty hunter houses. [[Scay Danson]], a member of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]], wrote a report on the possible threat posed by the Bounty Hunters' Guild, including the Mantis Syndicate. At that point, the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] sociopath [[Cradossk/Legends|Cradossk]], not affiliated with Mantis, was identified as the maximum authority of the Guild.<ref name="UG" />

Circa [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], the Black Bha'lir had infiltrated one agent into the Mantis Syndicate. Thus, when Mantis decided to send a bounty hunter after [[Karl Mathieu Ancher]], a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and member of the Bha'lir, the informant reported to [[The Tribunal]], the leaders of the Bha'lir. According to the tip, the hunter, known only as [[Kael (bounty hunter)|Kael]], planned to approach Ancher during the [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]] service of a notorious [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler on Socorro. The Tribunal knew nothing about Kael save for the individual's name, and as such Kael could easily approach Ancher undetected as a part of the crowd that was expected to attend. Additionally, The Tribunal wanted to solve the situation without Ancher's knowledge, so it asked a small group to find and stop Kael.<ref name="BS" />

At approximately the same time, [[High Lord]] [[Jaris Affric]],<ref name="GG10" /> Imperial [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the [[Sarin sector/Legends|Sarin sector]] and owner of several inherited, corporate, and criminal assets in that area,<ref name="SM" /> hired and kept on retainer most of the Syndicate.<ref name="GG10" /><ref name="GG9" /> Although the Syndicate still tracked criminals wanted by either the Empire or private corporations, most of its business came from bounties posted by Affric either directly or indirectly through any company under his control.<ref name="GG10" /> Affric used the Syndicate as his private army<ref name="GG9" /> of special operatives. He sent its members to track criminal organizations, units of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] active in his area,<ref name="SM" /> pirate groups, [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]], outlaw mercenary units, and any other collective hampering Affric's activities.<ref name="GG9" /> As Affric controlled most of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], there were many such groups in his sights.<ref name="SM" />

[[File:FennRizaar.jpg|thumb|left|Fenn Rizaar, former member of the Mantis Syndicate]]
This situation centered the range of the Syndicate in the Sarin sector, allowing it to offer only a [[Company/Legends|company]] of [[Troop/Legends|troops]],<ref name="GG9" /> similar to a special operations unit,<ref name="SM" /> for operations outside the sector. The group was successful in serving Affric and, within two years, had defeated four mercenary units, three swoop gangs, and one group of unauthorized [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] operating in the [[Suhuurmin system/Legends|Suhuurmin system]].<ref name="GG9" />

While under Affric's control, the Syndicate was contacted by Imperial [[Prefect/Legends|prefect]] [[Dengless Rinn]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kallistas]]. Rinn wanted to hire a company of mercenaries, including specialists in demolitions, to stage a coup against his boss, Governor [[Linrec]], in a scheme also involving weaponry and [[fission bomb]]s. Mantis arranged a meeting between Rinn and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] smuggler [[Tarquin Zian]], who was able to provide him with the weapons. Rinn and Mantis then started a three-[[Standard week/Legends|week]] negotiation to make a deal. Once they had reached an agreement, Mantis sent its troops to Kallistas while Rinn prepared a secret camp for them in a Kallistas [[Desert/Legends|desert]].<ref name="TP">{{WEGCite|story=Two for the Price of One|book=Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters}}</ref> Rinn's revolution was botched, and the prefect became a fugitive of the Empire.<ref name="GG11">''[[Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations]]''</ref>

Serving Affric, the Mantis Syndicate obtained wealth, power,<ref name="GG9" /> and [[Politics/Legends|political]] influence within the Empire, to the point of not having to worry about the legality of its own activities.<ref name="GG10" /> In addition, Affric did not care about the legality of the assignments he gave the organization.<ref name="SM" /> The Syndicate's prestige did not impress non-member bounty hunters, who considered the group to only be Affric's thugs-for-hire.<ref name="GG10" /> Other bounty hunter guilds admitted that the Mantis Syndicate was specialized in "acquiring" big groups, but still considered the Syndicate to be more a mercenary unit than a bounty hunter syndicate.<ref name="SM" /> This was a widespread opinion even outside the Guild.<ref name="TFU" /> Due to their closeness with Affric, the guild kept a close watch on the Mantis Syndicate.<ref name="EotE">[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]]</ref>

In the following years, Affric began to use the Mantis Syndicate in regular fights against troops of the Alliance, and also against the mercenary group [[Churhee's Riflemen]] in an attempt to capture [[Maydla Churhee]], the [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] of the organization's [[Vlaydm Churhee|leader]]. Circa [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Affric was short of money and got into debts with the Syndicate. When Lady Mantis noticed that Affric was unable pay her, she withdrew her men against his wishes. Lady Mantis managed the Syndicate as it continued to operate in Sarin, although its members were more prone to work for anyone paying her fees instead of working for Affric. She also reduced the fees to appeal to a wider range of potential clients.<ref name="SM" />

One of the new customers was [[Valis Lorn]], a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] and the Provisional Governor of the planet [[Andasala]]. Lorn wanted to establish Andasala as a neutral ground welcoming both agents of the Empire and of its rival government, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], as long as they did not use Andasala to fight. Needing more strength than his usual minions, Lorn hired Mantis mercenaries as his own law enforcement corps. The Mantis Syndicate was not enough to cement Lorn's position, but the Governor had already planned several other measures that helped him reach that goal.<ref name="GG11" />

During the following years, the New Republic took control of the Sarin sector and occasionally hired the Mantis Syndicate to track wanted Imperial agents. The New Republic was satisfied with the talent, professionalism, and political apathy of the Syndicate, although the government was still wary about the organization. In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zgorth'sth]] of the New Republic's [[Department of Threat Assessment]] filed a detailed report on the Mantis Syndicate. Zgorth'sth recommended high-ranking officers to keep contact with the Syndicate, because he felt the Syndicate was useful should the Republic need to obtain trained bounty hunters. He also believed that the Syndicate would observe the law if it was monitored.<ref name="SM" />

==Notable members==
[[File:Mantis_Syndicate.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mantis Syndicate members]]
{{Quote|Mantis "hunters" are just mercenary gangsters. Beats me how they're Guild affiliates.|Dengar|<ref name="Code" />}}
The owner and manager of the Syndicate was the amoral Lady Marina Mantis, who offered the services of her troops to anyone paying.<ref name="GG10" /> Individual bounty hunters in the Syndicate included Fenn Rizaar and Kael.<ref name="BS" /> During the height of the Galactic Civil War, however, the Syndicate employed no less than 985 professional hunters, 845 people as support personnel, and 220 administrative staff. Its assets were valued at fifty-four billion credits, including seven billion credits in cash.<ref name="GG10" /> The Syndicate was bigger than many of its peer houses, such as the [[Ragnar Syndicate]], but it also focused on bigger-scale activities.<ref name="GG9" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mantis Syndicate was first mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] guide ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' ([[1993]]), written for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Information on it was expanded in ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' ([[1994]]), another role-playing guide, which included a role-playing adventure, ''[[Two for the Price of One]]'', in which the Mantis Syndicate played a part. The Mantis Syndicate was further explored in later roleplaying supplements.

Only one source, ''Galaxy Guide 10,'' uses the longer name "Mantis Bounty Hunter Syndicate," with all the others systematically calling the organization the "Mantis Syndicate" even in formal situations. None of the sources specify whether either of the names is more formal than the other, so this article therefore makes use of the more widely used "Mantis Syndicate" name.
